title: Infrastructure-as-code drift — applied state diverges from merged plan
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the validity of the merged plan itself.
evidence: Saved search 'iac_drift' (every 30 min) compares cloud inventory against the last-merged IaC plan checksum and lists divergences.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CM-6 configuration settings require that the applied state match the approved baseline.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST-800-53 CM-6 (Configuration settings) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.42.5: Infrastructure-as-code drift — applied state diverges from merged plan.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.42.5' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CC8.1 includes drift detection as an observable outcome of change management.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SOC-2 CC8.1 (Change management) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.42.5: Infrastructure-as-code drift — applied state diverges from merged plan.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.42.5' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.8.9 configuration management requires monitoring of drift from baselines.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO-27001 A.8.9 (Configuration management (2022 new)) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.42.5: Infrastructure-as-code drift — applied state diverges from merged plan.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.42.5' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A manual console change alters a security-group rule that was established via Terraform; the next drift scan finds the difference.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All applied changes carry the current plan's checksum; no alert.

dataSources: Terraform/OpenTofu state snapshots, cloud provider inventory (AWS Config, Azure Resource Graph, GCP Asset Inventory), CI/CD merge events.
spl:
index=iac sourcetype=tf:state_snapshot earliest=-30m
| stats latest(plan_checksum) as expected_checksum by resource_id
| join resource_id [search index=cloud sourcetype=aws:config OR sourcetype=azure:rg OR sourcetype=gcp:asset earliest=-30m | stats latest(state_checksum) as actual_checksum by resource_id]
| where expected_checksum!=actual_checksum
| table resource_id expected_checksum actual_checksum
description: Continuously reconciles the cloud resource inventory against the last-approved IaC plan checksum and alerts on drift. Drift is a canonical change-management deficiency and also a known attacker-persistence pattern.
value: Turns 'we use Terraform' into a continuously-verified statement.
implementation: (1) Export per-plan checksums from CI on merge; (2) enable cloud inventory export; (3) run every 30 min; (4) SOAR auto-remediates via `terraform plan && apply` if allowed. Indexes required: `index=cloud`, `index=iac`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=aws:config`, `sourcetype=azure:rg`, `sourcetype=gcp:asset`, `sourcetype=tf:state_snapshot`.
visualization: Heatmap of resources × drift, table of drifting resources, single-value '% of resources matching plan'. Key fields: `actual_checksum`, `expected_checksum`, `resource_id`, `resource_id]`.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1562.001 Impair Defenses: Disable Tools

knownFalsePositives:
1. **Authorised compliance operations** — The compliance and governance team performs routine control testing, evidence gathering, and policy reviews that generate legitimate activity matching this use case's detection patterns. These operations are documented in the compliance programme calendar and should be excluded by the compliance-ops service account tag.

2. **Scheduled system maintenance windows** — Approved maintenance activities including patching, upgrades, and configuration changes produce expected control deviations during documented change windows that are pre-approved through the change advisory board (CAB).

3. **External audit and assessment activities** — During periodic audits (internal or external), assessors perform sampling, testing, and evidence collection activities that generate unusual access patterns covered by the engagement letter and audit notification.

4. **Third-party service provider operations** — Contracted service providers performing legitimate operations under signed agreements generate expected activity that may trigger monitoring rules during normal service delivery windows.

**Suppression mechanism:** Maintain a `compliance_exception_register` KV Store lookup containing approved maintenance windows, audit engagement periods, service provider identifiers, and compliance team service accounts. The correlation search joins this lookup and suppresses matches where `exception_status=approved` and the exception remains within its validity period.
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

• **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
• **Technology Add-on**: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase app ID 1876) must be installed on search heads and indexers. Verify with `| rest /services/apps/local | search label="Splunk Add-on for AWS" | table label version`.
• **Indexes**: `iac`, `cloud` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
• **Search-head capacity**: Subsearch-based detections need `subsearch_maxtime` ≥ 60s and `subsearch_maxout` ≥ 50000 in `limits.conf`. For high-volume environments, tune `[search] max_searches_per_cpu` up from default.
• **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `CM-6`. The control objective is: "Evidence that NIST-800-53 CM-6 (Configuration settings) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.42.5: Infrastructure-as-code drift — applied state diverges from merged plan." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is performed by the Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase app ID 1876), landing events in `iac`, `cloud` under `tf:state_snapshot`, `aws:config`, `azure:rg`, `gcp:asset`. CloudTrail management events average 5000-50000 API calls/account/hour for active workloads (~5-50 GB/account/month). Data events (S3, Lambda) can multiply this 10-100x.

Configure the input through Settings > Data Inputs > CloudTrail (SQS-based ingest is strongly preferred over S3 polling). A reference `inputs.conf` stanza looks like:

```ini
[aws_cloudtrail://prod_account]
aws_account = production
aws_region = us-east-1
sqs_queue = splunk-ct-queue
index = aws
sourcetype = aws:cloudtrail
```

The following fields are authoritative for this detection once the TA parses them: `eventName, eventSource, userIdentity.arn, sourceIPAddress, requestParameters, responseElements, errorCode, userAgent`. Verify they are extracted at search time with `index=iac earliest=-15m | fieldsummary` before scheduling the detection.


Four distinct operational considerations shape this detection's rollout:


• **Hint 1:** Export per-plan checksums from CI on merge. Report-generation cadence must match the regulatory reporting cycle; a monthly digest satisfies an annual review requirement but not a quarterly one.

• **Hint 2:** enable cloud inventory export. Report-generation cadence must match the regulatory reporting cycle; a monthly digest satisfies an annual review requirement but not a quarterly one.

• **Hint 3:** run every 30 min. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 4:** SOAR auto-remediates via `terraform plan && apply` if allowed. Automated remediation must be gated behind a defined approval workflow; compliance controls that take destructive action without human review are themselves a governance risk.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "Infrastructure-as-code drift — applied state diverges from merged plan," the aggregation produces the result shape required by the NIST-800-53 control — each row is a summary record the regulation expects to be presentable at audit time.

This search correlates `index=iac` (sourcetype=`tf:state_snapshot`) on the outer side with `index=cloud` (sourcetype=`aws:config`) on the inner side, joined on `resource_id` across the last 30 minutes. `type=inner` means the search only returns rows where `resource_id` exists in both datasets. This is the correct choice when the detection requires presence in both sources — a left-join would include orphaned rows that cannot be evaluated against the compliance rule.

Join caveat: Splunk's `| join` subsearch is capped at `max_searches_per_cpu * subsearch_maxout` results (default: 50000). If the inner subsearch exceeds this, rows are silently dropped and the resulting report will under-count. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd log_level=WARN subsearch` for truncation warnings. For inner searches expected to return >10000 rows, rewrite as `| stats` with a larger base search and post-filter, or use `| tstats` if a CIM datamodel covers the data.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.42.5: Infrastructure-as-code drift — applied state diverges from m]
cron_schedule = 0 */6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -30m
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.42.5,reg=NIST-800-53
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=iac sourcetype=tf:state_snapshot earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `resource_id`, `expected_checksum`, `actual_checksum`. Run:

```spl
index=iac sourcetype=tf:state_snapshot earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table resource_id, expected_checksum, actual_checksum
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.42.5: NIST-800-53 evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Heatmap Of Resources × Drift (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 2 — Table Of Drifting Resources (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 3 — Single-Value '% Of Resources Matching Plan' (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**No results when violations exist:** The most likely cause is subsearch truncation (see Step 2). Inspect `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=searchprocessor log_level=WARN subsearch` for messages like 'Output result count exceeded 50000.'

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**NIST-800-53-specific failure modes:**

• Control baseline selection: NIST 800-53 has Low/Moderate/High/Privacy baselines via FIPS 199 categorisation. A detection written to a High baseline threshold produces false alarms against Low-categorised systems. Join with the system categorisation register `nist_800_53_system_categorization.csv` before thresholding.

• CA-7 continuous monitoring cadence: specifies 'ongoing' authorisation activities. Detections supporting CA-7 must produce metrics at the organisation's ConMon cadence (monthly is common for most agencies) — weekly fires are fine but not themselves evidence of monthly compliance.

• Control inheritance: in a cloud-hybrid deployment, some controls are inherited from the Cloud Service Provider. Detections must not flag 'missing' controls the CSP provides (documented in the SSP via Control Inheritance matrix); reference the SSP appendix and suppress matching findings.

• SP 800-53 Rev 5 restructure: controls were renumbered between Rev 4 and Rev 5. Detections that cite Rev 4 numbering (e.g. AC-2.12) may not match the SSP if the organisation has migrated. Store both Rev numbers in the UC metadata and reconcile.
grandmaExplanation: We help you see when configs drift from what you baselined, so only approved change moves through.
